name: AXS 4h Deep Reset Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: a deeper pullback resets directional pressure, value is reclaimed, and continuation resumes from a cleaner location than shallow touch-and-go entries
thesis: Cycle 86 wants trend reset continuation after a deeper pullback instead of shallow EMA-touch logic. AXS 4h is the best ownership lane inside the allowed basket because ETH is too chop-owned for continuation and TAO has already punished late continuation entries. AXS offers a structurally different transition lane where deeper reset continuation can be tested without inheriting those exact failure modes.
invalidation_condition: If AXS 4h deep resets are still too sparse or reclaimed resets fail to continue after entry, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price pulls back deeply enough to reset positioning, reclaims value and structure, and then continues in the dominant direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: A deeper reset can clear weak positioning and create a cleaner continuation location than shallow EMA-touch logic. The reclaim after reset is the structural proof of resumed directional control.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 4h is the best fit because it is the only remaining allowed lane with enough directional flexibility to test continuation without forcing pure chop or overused TAO continuation grammar.

indicator_role_map.ATR_14: reset depth threshold and risk sizing

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The reclaim may still happen too late in the recovery, turning continuation entries into local exhaustion buys.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but runners disappoint, take earlier partials before changing the reset depth logic.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than ETH mean-reversion ideas, but better asymmetry when the reset truly resumes direction.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 4h clears moderate density and deep-reset continuation materially outperforms prior late continuation families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or repeated reset-reclaim entries that fail before first target.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because a deeper reset plus simple reclaim should trade more often than delayed-retest continuation

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit structurally different lane for deeper reset continuation.
test_lanes.continue_if: AXS 4h shows enough reset-reclaim continuations with acceptable first-target expectancy.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: AXS 4h still undertrades or reset entries fail too quickly.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a few larger resumed-direction legs.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same reset grammar survives on the stronger continuation lane once entry quality is improved.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h retains some reset-reclaim continuation with improved expectancy.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h confirms the concept still arrives too late there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger continuation carry than AXS.

test_lanes.why_included: Useful control to confirm that the idea does not belong on a pure chop lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h still prints some orderly reset continuations.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept remains anti-chop there.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Sparse and smaller continuation than AXS.
